title: LLM-Coordination: Evaluating and Analyzing Multi-agent Coordination Abilities in Large Language Models
problem: Evaluating LLM coordination in pure coordination games
method: LLM-Coordination benchmark with Agentic Coordination CoordinationQA ToM reasoning and answer verification
dataset_or_env: Pure coordination games including Overcooked Hanabi CollabCapture CollabEscape
metrics: Task scores CoordinationQA accuracy and ablations
main_result: LLMs coordinate well in some settings but struggle with ToM and joint planning; verification helps Hanabi safety
limitation: Focuses on pure coordination and LLM answer verification rather than deterministic mixed-motive trace labels
relation_to_us: Shows verification-adjacent coordination work; our gap must emphasize rule-grounded verifier labels in mixed-motive dynamic-action play

title: Sparks of Cooperative Reasoning: LLMs as Strategic Hanabi Agents
problem: Evaluating LLMs as cooperative agents under incomplete information
method: Large-scale Hanabi LLM benchmark with Watson Sherlock Mycroft scaffolds plus HanabiLogs and HanabiRewards datasets
metrics: Game score cross-play move-level utilities annotated trajectories and downstream transfer
main_result: Strong reasoning models improve but trail humans and specialist agents; fine-tuning on HanabiRewards improves cooperative play
limitation: Fully cooperative and includes Hanabi-specific strategic communication and move utilities rather than mixed-motive rule-grounded trace verification
relation_to_us: Close on cooperative card-game traces and utilities but not team-vs-team Guandan verifier labels

title: M3-BENCH: Process-Aware Evaluation of LLM Agents Social Behaviors in Mixed-Motive Games
problem: Evaluating LLM social behavior in mixed-motive games
method: 24-game benchmark with BTA RPA and CCA views
metrics: Behavior trajectory reasoning process and communication content analysis
main_result: Process-aware metrics expose behavior hidden by outcome-only evaluation
limitation: Uses broad social labels and communication analysis rather than deterministic game-rule trace verification
relation_to_us: Strongest mixed-motive process-aware neighbor; our gap must be rule-grounded verification under dynamic legal actions

title: Explaining Decisions of Agents in Mixed-Motive Games
problem: Explaining agent decisions in mixed-motive games
method: SBUE probable-actions and SICA explanation methods
main_result: Addresses competition cheap-talk and implicit communication by actions
limitation: Human-facing explanations not LLM trace verifier metrics
relation_to_us: Related explanation baseline; our focus is structured trace checking and verifier labels

title: Evaluating and Enhancing LLMs Agent based on Theory of Mind in Guandan: A Multi-Player Cooperative Game under Imperfect Information
problem: Evaluating LLM agents in Guandan with ToM planning
method: ToM prompting plus RL-based action recommender
metrics: Game scores against random rule-based and DanZero+ baselines
main_result: ToM and action recommender improve LLM play but RL remains stronger
limitation: Already covers LLM Guandan zero-communication collaboration and dynamic action space
relation_to_us: Closest paper; our novelty cannot be LLM Guandan zero-communication or ToM but must be verifier-grounded reasoning-action diagnosis

title: DanZero: Mastering GuanDan Game with Reinforcement Learning
method: Distributed self-play deep Monte Carlo reinforcement learning with state-action features
metrics: Win rates against eight heuristic agents and human evaluation
main_result: DanZero beats rule-based baselines and reports human-level performance after large-scale training
limitation: Focuses on game strength and state-action value learning not LLM reasoning traces or verifier diagnostics
relation_to_us: Provides RL/game-strength baseline context but not our diagnostic target

title: OpenGuanDan: A Large-Scale Imperfect Information Game Benchmark
method: Efficient simulator and comprehensive agent evaluation
main_result: Introduces benchmark for learning-based and rule-based Guandan agents and supports LLM integration
limitation: Simulator benchmark overlaps on environment but not reasoning-trace verification
relation_to_us: Potential external environment or baseline; our benchmark is diagnostic and trace-verifier oriented

title: Communicating Activations Between Language Model Agents
main_result: Activation communication recovers much of zero-communication gap in coordination games
limitation: Studies adding communication rather than diagnosing action-only implicit signaling
relation_to_us: Useful contrast for why forbidding explicit communication is a stricter evaluation setting

title: CodeAgents: A Token-Efficient Framework for Codified Multi-Agent Reasoning in LLMs
method: Code-like modular prompting for multi-agent reasoning
main_result: Structured pseudocode makes multi-agent reasoning interpretable and verifiable
limitation: Not focused on games zero-communication mixed motives or rule-legal action verification
relation_to_us: Related to structured/verifiable reasoning but different task and verifier target

title: Game Theory Meets Large Language Models: A Systematic Survey with Taxonomy and New Frontiers
problem: Surveying game theory and LLM research
method: Taxonomy over game-based evaluation game-theoretic improvement event modeling and LLMs for game theory
main_result: Shows LLM game research is broad and crowded
limitation: Survey not a direct benchmark or verifier method
relation_to_us: Background source to avoid overbroad novelty claims

title: Say One Thing Do Another? Diagnosing Reasoning-Execution Gaps in VLM-Powered Mobile-Use Agents
problem: Diagnosing reasoning-execution mismatch in mobile-use agents
method: Ground-truth alignment diagnostic for VLM GUI agents
metrics: Reasoning quality execution accuracy and gap metrics
main_result: Scaling reduces but does not eliminate reasoning-execution gaps
limitation: Single-agent GUI setting rather than multi-agent mixed-motive games
relation_to_us: Important neighboring phrase; prevents claiming first reasoning-action consistency diagnosis

title: Strat-Reasoner: Reinforcing Strategic Reasoning of LLMs in Multi-Agent Games
problem: Improving LLM strategic reasoning in multi-agent games
method: RL framework with recursive reasoning centralized CoT comparison and hybrid advantage estimation
main_result: Reports average strategic performance improvements across multi-agent games
limitation: Focuses on RL training and two-player alternating games rather than deterministic trace verification in Guandan
relation_to_us: Strong neighboring work for LLM strategic reasoning in games; our claim must emphasize diagnostic verifier labels not RL optimization

title: How Far Are LLMs from Professional Poker Players? Revisiting Game-Theoretic Reasoning with Agentic Tool Use
problem: Evaluating and improving LLM game-theoretic reasoning under uncertainty
method: ToolPoker with solver-assisted reasoning traces behavior cloning and regret-inspired RL
main_result: Finds knowing-doing gaps and improves GTO-style reasoning with external tools
limitation: Single-player-at-decision poker framing and solver grounding rather than team-vs-team zero-communication Guandan verifier labels
relation_to_us: High terminology and hidden-information risk; supports our need to avoid claiming first reasoning-trace game diagnosis

title: Game Reasoning Arena
problem: Evaluating LLMs through strategic game playing
method: OpenSpiel-based multi-game arena with LLM vs random LLM vs LLM and self-play modes
dataset_or_env: Multiple games including tic-tac-toe connect four Kuhn poker prisoners dilemma matching pennies hex chess
main_result: Provides broad LLM strategic-game evaluation infrastructure
limitation: Repository framework rather than paper with rule-grounded structured trace verifier for Guandan
relation_to_us: Potential external benchmark context; our novelty should not be generic LLM game arena infrastructure
